thought.text: I will create the `bookings.yaml` blueprint file. This defines the `Booking` model, which belongs to an organization, a user, and a time slot. Staff can view and update bookings (status), while customers can create, view, update (cancel), and delete (destroy) their own bookings.

action.file_text:
model: Booking
slug: bookings
table: bookings

options:
  belongs_to_organization: true
  soft_deletes: false
  audit_trail: false
  pagination: true
  per_page: 25

columns:
  user_id:
    type: foreignId
    foreign_model: User
    nullable: false
    index: true
  time_slot_id:
    type: foreignId
    foreign_model: TimeSlot
    nullable: false
    index: true
  status:
    type: string
    default: "pending"
    nullable: false
    filterable: true
  notes:
    type: text
    nullable: true

relationships:
  - type: belongsTo
    model: Organization
  - type: belongsTo
    model: User
  - type: belongsTo
    model: TimeSlot

permissions:
  owner:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: "*"
    update_fields: "*"
  admin:
    actions: [index, show, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: []
    update_fields:
      - status
  staff:
    actions: [index, show, update]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields: []
    update_fields:
      - status
  customer:
    actions: [index, show, store, update, destroy]
    show_fields: "*"
    create_fields:
      - time_slot_id
      - notes
    update_fields:
      - status
      - notes

tool_call.name: guarded_file_editor
tool_call.arguments:
